supporting_works.title: A supplement to the pharmacopoeia : being a treatise on pharmacology in general; including not only the drugs and compounds which are used by practitioners of medicine, but also those which are sold by chemists, druggists, and herbalists, for other purposes; together with a collection of the most useful medical formulæ ; an explanation of the contractions used by physicians and druggists; the medical arrangement of the articles of the London pharmacopeia, with their doses, at one view; a similar list of the indigenous plants of the British islands, which are capable of being used in medicine, &c. ; and also a very copious index, English and Latin, of the various names by which the articles have been known at different periods. / by Samuel Frederick Gray.

Pharmacopoeia Londinensis, or, The London dispensatory : further adorned by the studies and collections of the fellows, now living in the said colledg : being that book by which all apothecaries are bound to make up all the medicines in their shops

A dictionary of domestic medicine and household surgery / by Spencer Thomson.
A dictionary of materia medica and practical pharmacy : including a translation of the formulae of the London pharmacopoeia / by William Thomas Brande.

A dictionary of medical science : containing a full explanation of the various subjects and terms of anatomy, physiology, medical chemistry, pharmacy, pharmacology, therapeutics, medicine, hygiene, dietetics, pathology, bacteriology, surgery, ophthalmology, otology, laryngology, dermatology, gynecology, obstetrics, pediatrics, medical jurisprudence, dentistry, etc
supporting_works.creator: Dunglison, Robley, 1798-1869.

Gray's supplement to the pharmacopoeia : being a concise but comprehensive dispensatory and manual of facts and formulae for the chemist and druggist and medical practioner / E. Theophilus Redwood.

retrieval_text: Broom is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 145 works and 249 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Broom, Broom Tops. Common plant-part co-mentions include Tops, Root, Seed, Bark, Flower, Leaf. Common preparation co-mentions include Decoction, Extract, Infusion, Juice, Powder, Oil. Common condition co-mentions include Dropsy, Rheumatism, Cold or catarrh, Vomiting or nausea, Epilepsy or convulsions, Fever. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Toxicity watch, Narcotic or sedative caution, Fatal-risk warning, Bleeding caution, Child-use caution. Representative source works include A supplement to the pharmacopoeia : being a treatise on pharmacology in general; including not only the drugs and compounds which are used by practitioners of medicine, but also those which are sold by chemists, druggists, and herbalists, for other purposes; together with a collection of the most useful medical formulæ ; an explanation of the contractions used by physicians and druggists; the medical arrangement of the articles of the London pharmacopeia, with their doses, at one view; a similar list of the indigenous plants of the British islands, which are capable of being used in medicine, &c. ; and also a very copious index, English and Latin, of the various names by which the articles have been known at different periods. / by Samuel Frederick Gray.; Pharmacopoeia Londinensis, or, The London dispensatory : further adorned by the studies and collections of the fellows, now living in the said colledg : being that book by which all apothecaries are bound to make up all the medicines in their shops; A cyclopedia of domestic medicine and surgery: being an alphabetical account of the various diseases incident to the human frame ... also, instructions for administering the various substances used in medicine; for the regulation of diet and regimen / by Thomas Andrew, with contributions by several members of the Royal College of Surgeons, Edinburgh.; A dictionary of domestic medicine and household surgery / by Spencer Thomson.. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
